item.title: Viktor Orbán Could Actually Lose
item.description: The Hungarian leader faces an energized opposition—and questions about whether he would accept defeat.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: How Did Samuel Alito Become This Angry?
item.description: A quiet, bookish justice’s personal leanings have become ever more overt.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Canellos

item.title: A Different Moon From the One We’ve Known
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission is bringing home a view of the moon unlike any humanity has seen before.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boyle

item.title: The Iran War Showed a New Side of Pope Leo
item.description: Don’t call him the quiet pope anymore.
item.dc:creator: Francis X. Rocca

item.title: Photos of the Week: Solar Eclipse, Amazon Monument, Titanic Bonfire
item.description: Blossoming cherry trees in Germany, a fertility and harvest festival in Poland, a horse harness parade in England, a Tomb-Sweeping Day festival in Hong Kong, and much more
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: The Great Travel Meltdown of 2026
item.description: Airports are suffering a perfect storm of actual problems and passenger anxieties.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Tiffany

item.title: The Banality of MAGA-fication
item.description: A new book by an unremarkable Republican accidentally illuminates the devolution of the party.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Is Schoolwork Optional Now?
item.description: Education is on the verge of becoming fully automated.
item.dc:creator: Lila Shroff

item.title: Ye Wants Your Forgiveness. So What?
item.description: The former Kanye West is making his bid to rejoin mainstream culture—with mixed results.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kornhaber

item.title: Vance’s ‘Fraud Czar’ Title May Come Back to Haunt Him
item.description: The vice president’s ambitious new focus comes with some baggage.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: What Will Humanity Do With the Moon?
item.description: As the Artemis missions work to build a permanent lunar home, we should remember why we keep going back.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Bai

item.title: Reasons to be Hopeful in Iran
item.description: How a deal could change the country for the better.
item.dc:creator: Arash Azizi

item.title: Claude Mythos Is Everyone’s Problem
item.description: What happens when AI can hack everything?
item.dc:creator: Matteo Wong

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Cities, Islands, and Island Cities
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: A Cancer Treatment That Does More Than Scientists Thought
item.description: CAR-T cell therapy, originally developed for cancer, is showing ever more promise as a treatment for autoimmune diseases.
item.dc:creator: Katherine J. Wu

item.title: Masterpieces of the New Deal
item.description: Critics wrote the work off as kitsch for the masses. But a set of murals celebrating Social Security—now threatened with destruction—show that such sweeping judgments went too far.
item.dc:creator: Judith Shulevitz

item.title: A Writer of ‘Hypnotic Repetition’
item.description: With her first new novel in more than 20 years, Nancy Lemann returns, yet again, to New Orleans and its eccentricities.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Tiffany

item.title: What China Just Learned From the Iran War
item.description: A blockade of Taiwan would hurt the global economy more than Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Simon Shuster

item.title: Winners of the 2026 World Press Photo Contest
item.description: The winning entries of this year’s World Press Photo Contest were just announced. This year, according to organizers, 57,376 images were submitted for judging, made by 3,747 photographers from 141 countries.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: States Are Learning the Wrong Lesson From the ‘Mississippi Miracle’
item.description: A phonics-based curriculum is only one part of how Mississippi went from worst to first in education. The other part is much harder to pull off.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Canter
